title: Smoky Gooey Mexican Banana Pibil
description: A smoky, one-pot Mexican dish featuring banana, temper, and anti-inflammatory-friendly ingredients. Inspired by cochinita pibil, this recipe is a unique twist on traditional flavors.

ingredients.name: 4 ripe plantain bananas
ingredients.notes: medium-sized bananas will work best for this recipe

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup lard or vegetable shortening
ingredients.notes: keep in mind that using lard will add a richer flavor to the dish

ingredients.name: 2 cloves garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: do not mince too fine, as it will burn in the heat

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon ground cumin
ingredients.notes: use a high quality ground cumin for the best flavor

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon dried oregano
ingredients.notes: Mexican oregano is best for this recipe, but Spanish oregano works too

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon ground cayenne pepper
ingredients.notes: use less if you prefer a milder heat

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon salt
ingredients.notes: use kosher or sea salt for a more complex flavor

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup orange juice
ingredients.notes: freshly squeezed is best, but bottled will work in a pinch

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons chopped fresh cilantro
ingredients.notes: optional, but adds a nice freshness to the dish

instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat the lard or shortening over medium-high heat until it reaches 400°F (200°C).
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the ground cumin and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the dried oregano and cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly.
Add the ground cayenne pepper and cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly.
Add the sliced plantain bananas and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are nicely charred.
Add the orange juice, salt, and chopped cilantro (if using) to the pot. Stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 10-15 minutes, or until the bananas are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before serving.
Note: if using lard, it's best to let the dish cool completely before refrigerating or freezing to prevent the sauce from becoming too solid.
